flashcards.back: A PDA is a computational model that extends a finite automaton by adding an unbounded stack memory with LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) behavior, enabling recognition of context-free languages.

flashcards.front: What are the seven components of a formal PDA definition?
flashcards.back: Q (states), Σ (input alphabet), Γ (stack alphabet), δ (transition function), q₀ (start state), Z₀ (initial stack symbol), F (accepting/final states).

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the stack alphabet Γ in a PDA?
flashcards.back: The stack alphabet Γ is the set of symbols that can be pushed onto or popped from the stack, including the initial stack symbol Z₀.

flashcards.front: What does the transition function δ in a PDA define?
flashcards.back: δ: Q × (Σ ∪ {ε}) × Γ → P(Q × Γ*) defines how the PDA moves from one configuration to another based on current state, input symbol (or ε), and top of stack.

flashcards.front: What are the two methods of acceptance in a PDA?
flashcards.back: 1) Acceptance by Final State: Reach an accepting state after consuming all input. 2) Acceptance by Empty Stack: Stack becomes empty after consuming all input.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between deterministic and non-deterministic PDA?
flashcards.back: A DPDA has at most one choice for any configuration (δ(q,a,X) has at most one element), while an NPDA can have multiple possible moves from the same configuration.

flashcards.front: What is an instantaneous description (ID) in a PDA?
flashcards.back: A triple (q, w, α) representing the current state q, remaining input w, and current stack contents α. It describes the complete configuration of the PDA at any moment.

flashcards.front: What is ε-move (or ε-transition) in a PDA?
flashcards.back: A transition that consumes no input symbol (ε), allowing the PDA to change state and modify the stack without reading any input character.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the initial stack symbol Z₀?
flashcards.back: Z₀ marks the bottom of the stack and serves as a reference point; it helps the PDA know when the stack is empty during empty-stack acceptance.

flashcards.front: What is stack replacement operation in PDA?
flashcards.back: Instead of just push or pop, the PDA can replace the top stack symbol X with a string γ (pushdown of γ), enabling complex pattern matching.

flashcards.front: Why can PDAs recognize languages that finite automata cannot?
flashcards.back: The unbounded stack provides infinite memory capacity in a restricted way (LIFO), enabling matching of nested structures like balanced parentheses.

flashcards.front: What is the language recognized by a PDA?
flashcards.back: The set of all strings that cause the PDA to reach an accepting configuration (final state or empty stack) after consuming the entire input.

flashcards.front: What is a configuration in PDA terminology?
flashcards.back: A configuration represents the complete state of the PDA at any point, consisting of the current state, remaining input, and current stack contents.

flashcards.front: What is the notation for PDA move?
flashcards.back: (p, a, X) ⊢ (q, ε, α) means: from state p, reading 'a', with X on top of stack, move to state q and replace X with α on stack.

flashcards.front: What is the relationship between PDA and Context-Free Languages?
flashcards.back: Non-deterministic PDAs can recognize exactly the class of Context-Free Languages (CFLs); deterministic PDAs recognize a proper subset (deterministic CFLs).
